items.title: Stripe is best modeled as programmable financial infrastructure, not only checkout software
items.summary: Stripe's public positioning combines payments, billing, connect, tax, and financial workflow products into one operating layer for internet businesses.
items.implication: Model merchant payments, recurring revenue, and platform money movement together because the customer economics compound across them.

items.title: Checkout conversion is increasingly a network and orchestration problem
items.summary: Stripe positions Elements and its payments stack around higher conversion through wallets, saved details, and local method orchestration rather than basic card form acceptance alone.
items.implication: Treat checkout UX, payment-method selection, and authorization optimization as one conversion loop.

items.title: Recurring revenue infrastructure is becoming strategic, not adjacent
items.summary: Stripe's public narrative and product surface show Billing, Tax, and finance automation as major levers for software and AI-native businesses, not side modules.
items.implication: Model pricing, invoicing, recovery, tax, and revenue recognition as one recurring-revenue operating system.

items.title: Platforms increasingly want embedded fintech without building a fintech stack
items.summary: Stripe Connect is positioned around onboarding, payouts, and multi-party funds flows so platforms can monetize and scale counterparties without bespoke regulated infrastructure.
items.implication: Keep connected-account onboarding, funds routing, payouts, and reconciliation tightly linked in the domain model.

items.title: Fraud control that damages conversion is now a growth problem
items.summary: Radar is explicitly positioned around improving fraud protection while preserving legitimate approvals, which means risk tooling is part of revenue capture rather than only loss prevention.
items.implication: Model fraud scoring, adaptive authentication, and dispute handling as growth-sensitive controls.

items.title: Cross-border monetization increasingly depends on built-in tax operations
items.summary: Stripe Tax focuses on obligation monitoring, registration support, and calculation, which shows tax is a launch blocker for global monetization rather than a back-office afterthought.
items.implication: Treat tax determination and registration workflows as a core monetization capability, especially for recurring businesses.

items.title: Unified commerce matters when operators want one payment and finance model
items.summary: Stripe Terminal extends the same platform into in-person payments, which is valuable when merchants want consistent reporting, refunds, and operations across channels.
items.implication: Model online and in-person acceptance as one merchant operating system rather than separate product lines.

items.title: Identity verification is becoming a product primitive for platform activation
items.summary: Stripe Identity and related onboarding products show that counterparty verification is not only a compliance step but a platform conversion lever.
items.implication: Keep identity verification close to connected-account lifecycle and payout activation workflows.
